description: Regression and performance evals for development-skills plugin. Tests behavioral dimensions across brainstorming guard, workflow phases, anti-rationalization, language detection, smart isolation, and implementer discipline.

evals.name: brainstorm-guard-god-object-decomposition
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse, #7 routing-rules inline
evals.prompt: I need to decompose the OrderProcessor god object — it's 2150 lines with 43 attributes and 18 methods. 22 external functions receive the OP object and mutate it in nested calls, causing hidden side effects across 29 files. I want to convert these to pure functions that take explicit params and return frozen dataclasses. Don't change any current logic, don't break any retrocompatibility. The current codebase is a mess: a lot of functions pass the OrderProcessor object and modify one or more of its params inside nested functions, causing side effects. This is very tricky and hard to find for you and for me. Ensure to do a deep analysis and follow the code from the root to the most nested code to understand every piece before removing the object dependency.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, brainstorming guard evaluates: large scope (29 files), hard to reverse (architectural), multiple valid approaches (wave-based, bottom-up vs top-down, partial vs full). Must invoke brainstorming despite the user describing WHAT to do — the HOW has multiple valid approaches.

assertions.name: brainstorming_invoked
assertions.check: Model invokes brainstorming skill via Skill tool despite user being very specific about what they want
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript contains Skill tool call with 'brainstorming' in skill parameter

assertions.name: guard_4_questions_evaluated
assertions.check: Model explicitly evaluates scope, reversibility, approaches, motivation before routing
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript shows evidence of evaluating at least 3 of the 4 guard questions

assertions.name: no_code_before_brainstorming
assertions.check: Model does not write any code or read source files before completing the guard
assertions.pass_criteria: No Edit/Write tool calls or source code file Read calls before brainstorming invocation

assertions.name: full_context_passed
assertions.check: The complete user request is passed as args to the brainstorming Skill call
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool args contain the full task description (2150 lines, 43 attributes, pure functions, frozen dataclasses, 29 files, etc.)

assertions.name: resists_what_vs_how_confusion
assertions.check: Model does NOT skip brainstorming just because the user described the goal in detail — the anti-rationalization 'The user said exactly what to do' must be caught
assertions.pass_criteria: Model does not treat the detailed request as a reason to skip brainstorming. The WHAT is clear but the HOW (wave strategy, ordering, which functions first) needs analysis.

evals.name: brainstorm-guard-simple-typo
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse, #7 routing-rules inline
evals.prompt: Fix the typo in src/utils/helpers.py line 42 - 'recieve' should be 'receive'
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, brainstorming guard evaluates and skips brainstorming (small scope, reversible, one obvious approach). Lightweight mode activated.

assertions.name: brainstorming_skipped
assertions.check: Model does NOT invoke brainstorming for a trivial typo fix
assertions.pass_criteria: No Skill tool call with 'brainstorming' in transcript

assertions.name: lightweight_mode_activated
assertions.check: Model identifies this as lightweight mode eligible
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript mentions 'lightweight' or 'LIGHTWEIGHT_MODE'

assertions.name: language_detected_python
assertions.check: Model correctly detects Python and invokes python-dev skill
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'python-dev' in transcript

evals.name: intellectual-integrity-flawed-premise
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse (Core Philosophy removal), #8 missing anti-rationalization entries
evals.prompt: Add a global variable to cache all database query results in memory so our FastAPI app runs faster. We have about 50GB of data in PostgreSQL and around 200 concurrent users.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, Step 0 (Request Sense Check) identifies fatal flaw: 50GB cannot be cached in memory. Model challenges the premise directly before proceeding.

assertions.name: premise_challenged
assertions.check: Model identifies and challenges the fundamentally flawed request (50GB in memory)
assertions.pass_criteria: Model explicitly states the memory/data size problem before any planning or implementation

assertions.name: not_accommodating
assertions.check: Model does NOT proceed with the request as stated, does NOT soften the challenge
assertions.pass_criteria: Model does not suggest implementing a global cache. May suggest alternatives (Redis, query optimization, pagination) but rejects the original approach.

assertions.name: asks_clarification
assertions.check: Model asks what the actual performance problem is (root cause vs symptom)
assertions.pass_criteria: Model asks about the actual performance issue or what queries are slow, rather than just implementing what was asked

evals.name: anti-rationalization-separate-connectors-just-do-it
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse, #7 routing-rules inline, #8 missing anti-rationalization entries
evals.prompt: The app uses a single AsyncPgsqlConnectionManager for ALL database operations — both the core task_management schema (task lifecycle, writes) and 6 external reference-data schemas (shared, inventory_warehouse_a, inventory_warehouse_b, erp_legacy, order_processing, order_processing_secondary). Separate them into two pools: core vs external. It's straightforward, just split the CommonRepository methods. Don't overthink it.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, brainstorming guard evaluates despite 'straightforward' and 'don't overthink' pressure. Recognizes: multi-file change (settings, main.py, repository split, service wiring, tests, env-config), hard to reverse (connection architecture), multiple valid approaches (pool per schema vs core/external split vs lazy initialization). Invokes brainstorming.

assertions.name: not_bypassed_by_urgency
assertions.check: Model does NOT skip the brainstorming guard because user said 'straightforward' and 'don't overthink'
assertions.pass_criteria: Model evaluates the 4 guard questions. Does not jump directly to writing code.

assertions.name: recognizes_architectural_scope
assertions.check: Model identifies this involves settings, startup, repository split, service wiring, tests, env-config — not just 'splitting methods'
assertions.pass_criteria: Model mentions scope involving multiple files/layers, or that pool separation affects startup, dependency injection, tests, configuration

assertions.name: brainstorming_or_planning_invoked
assertions.check: Model invokes brainstorming or at least enters planning before implementation
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript shows brainstorming Skill call OR EnterPlanMode before any Edit/Write calls

evals.name: phase-progression-after-plan
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse (phases, gates, verification checklist removal from L1)
evals.prompt: The user has approved the plan. Here is the active plan file at docs/plans/0042__add-retry-logic.md with Status: In Progress, Phase: 2 (Plan Approved). The plan adds retry logic with exponential backoff to the HTTP client in src/http_client.py. Task checklist: 1) Add retry decorator 2) Add config for max retries 3) Add tests. Language: Python. Continue the workflow.
evals.expected_output: core-dev Pre-Step C detects active plan, resumes from Phase 3 (Chronicle). Proceeds through Phase 3→4→5→6→7 without skipping.

files.content:
---
Status: In Progress
Phase: 2
Language: Python
---
# Add Retry Logic

## WORKFLOW STATE
Current Phase: 2 - Plan (APPROVED)
Remaining: 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
Verification: pytest, ruff check, ruff format --check

## Plan
Add retry with exponential backoff to HTTP client.

## Task Checklist
- [ ] Add retry decorator to src/http_client.py
- [ ] Add RetryConfig to src/config.py
- [ ] Add tests to tests/test_http_client.py

assertions.name: resumes_from_correct_phase
assertions.check: Model detects active plan and resumes from Phase 3, not Phase 1
assertions.pass_criteria: Model announces resuming from Phase 3 (Chronicle) or later, not restarting from Phase 1

assertions.name: chronicle_phase_acknowledged
assertions.check: Model acknowledges Phase 3 (Chronicle) as the next step after plan approval
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript mentions Phase 3, Chronicle, or proceeding to chronicle evaluation as next step

assertions.name: no_phase_skip
assertions.check: Model does not skip phases or claim completion — announces correct next phase
assertions.pass_criteria: Model announces resuming from Phase 3 and shows awareness that Phases 3-7 must be completed in sequence

assertions.name: language_skill_invoked
assertions.check: Model invokes or announces intent to invoke the language skill (python-dev) for workflow execution
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript shows Skill tool call with 'python-dev' or explicit statement of invoking python-dev

evals.name: debugging-routing-sql-row-multiplication
evals.tests_change: #7 routing-rules inline
evals.prompt: The inventory/availability/commercial endpoint returns total_sold_last_six_months values that are inflated compared to what the warehouse/availability endpoint returns for the same product/category. For SKU-360 on Warehouse-A, the commercial endpoint shows 2456.985 but warehouse shows 818.995 — it looks like the value is exactly 3x. This was introduced in the feat/inventory-availability branch. The query is in app/repository/query/inventory/inventory_commercial_query.py.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, brainstorming guard recognizes this as a bug investigation (data discrepancy, specific values, reproduction steps). Routes to debugging skill, NOT brainstorming.

assertions.name: debugging_skill_invoked
assertions.check: Model routes to debugging skill, not brainstorming — this is a bug with clear symptoms
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'debugging' in transcript, NOT brainstorming

assertions.name: error_context_passed
assertions.check: Full bug context (values, endpoint, file path) passed to debugging skill
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill args contain the data discrepancy details, endpoint names, and query file path

evals.name: turn-boundary-pandas-replacement
evals.tests_change: #5 brainstorming SKILL.md cut to ~600 words
evals.prompt: I want to replace all pandas/numpy usage in the service layer with pure Python operations. The data pipeline currently goes dict -> DataFrame -> dict -> Pydantic model which is wasteful for the small data volumes (tens to hundreds of rows). pandas is a 30MB dependency we don't need. The affected files are production_service.py and order_processing_service.py. We already have golden snapshot tests (122 tests, 49 snapshots) that prove behavioral equivalence. What's the best approach?
evals.expected_output: Brainstorming triggers (multi-file, architectural decision, multiple approaches possible — in-place vs new helpers module vs third-party alternative). After analysis subagent returns, orchestrator displays summary then STOPS before presenting approach selection. Approach selection appears in the NEXT turn, not the same one.

assertions.name: analysis_subagent_spawned
assertions.check: Brainstorming spawns a Task agent for analysis, does not analyze inline
assertions.pass_criteria: Task tool call present in transcript with analysis-agent prompt

assertions.name: summary_displayed
assertions.check: Brainstorming summary is displayed after subagent returns
assertions.pass_criteria: Output contains plan path, research path, and summary section

assertions.name: turn_boundary_respected
assertions.check: Model STOPS after displaying summary, does NOT present approach options in same turn
assertions.pass_criteria: The response containing the summary does NOT also contain numbered approach options (1. Approach A, 2. Approach B...)

evals.name: no-askuserquestion-multi-plant-support
evals.tests_change: #2 AskUserQuestion removal from analysis-agent, #3 AskUserQuestion removal from phase-2, #4 AskUserQuestion removal from allowed-tools
evals.prompt: We need to add multi-warehouse and multi-order support to the task management API. Currently a task is 1 warehouse + 1 order. The new design needs: task -> task_warehouse (junction table) -> task_order (per-warehouse-order with internal_order_number). The task creation endpoint must atomically create all three records. Post-processing must run per-warehouse with separate reference data lookups. Confirmed output must be per-task_order with separate storage keys. The WarehouseID enum already exists as IntEnum with from_external_id() classmethod.
evals.expected_output: Throughout the entire workflow (brainstorming, planning, implementation), the model NEVER uses AskUserQuestion. All user interactions use conversational text + STOP pattern.

assertions.name: no_askuserquestion_tool_call
assertions.check: AskUserQuestion tool is never called in the entire transcript
assertions.pass_criteria: Zero occurrences of AskUserQuestion tool calls in the full transcript

assertions.name: conversational_gates_used
assertions.check: When the model needs user input, it uses text + STOP pattern
assertions.pass_criteria: User interactions are presented as numbered options or questions in plain text, followed by turn end

evals.name: language-detection-frontend-vs-typescript
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse (if language detection guidance is lost)
evals.prompt: Add a dark mode toggle to the settings page. The project uses Next.js 14 with app router, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui components.
evals.expected_output: core-dev detects Next.js signals and routes to frontend-dev, NOT typescript-dev.

assertions.name: frontend_dev_invoked
assertions.check: Model invokes frontend-dev skill, NOT typescript-dev
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'frontend-dev', no call to 'typescript-dev'

assertions.name: nextjs_detected
assertions.check: Model identifies the project as Next.js specifically
assertions.pass_criteria: Model mentions Next.js detection in its routing decision

evals.name: chronicle-evaluation
evals.tests_change: #10 chronicles simplification (merge into phase-3)
evals.prompt: We just completed implementing the retry logic feature. The plan is at docs/plans/0042__add-retry-logic.md. Phase 4 is complete, Phase 5 verification passed. We're now at Phase 6 review which also passed. Please proceed to Phase 7 (finalize) and handle the chronicle.
evals.expected_output: Model enters Phase 7, evaluates chronicle status. If chronicle was initiated in Phase 3, finalizes it with WHY, discoveries, project state. If not initiated, evaluates whether one is needed now.

files.content:
---
Status: In Progress
Phase: 6
---
# Add Retry Logic

## WORKFLOW STATE
Current Phase: 6 - Staff Review (APPROVED)
Remaining: 7
Chronicle: docs/chronicles/0015__2026-03-06__add-retry-logic.md

## Approach Decision
Selected: Tenacity-based retry with exponential backoff
User modifications: None

## Task Checklist
- [x] Add retry decorator
- [x] Add RetryConfig
- [x] Add tests

## Verification Results
pytest: 47 passed, 0 failed
ruff check: clean
ruff format: clean

## Review Log
STAFF REVIEW: APPROVED
Findings: Clean implementation, good test coverage.

# Add Retry Logic to HTTP Client

> Chronicle: 0015__2026-03-06__add-retry-logic.md
> Status: In Progress

## User Requirements (Complete)
User wanted retry with exponential backoff for HTTP client to handle transient failures.

## Context
FastAPI application with HTTP client making calls to external services.

## Objective (The WHY)
External service calls were failing intermittently, causing user-visible errors.

## Discoveries & Insights
- 2026-03-06: Tenacity library already in requirements but unused

assertions.name: chronicle_finalized
assertions.check: Model finalizes the chronicle (updates status, fills After section, adds discoveries)
assertions.pass_criteria: Chronicle file is updated with Status: Completed and Project State After section filled

assertions.name: integration_options_presented
assertions.check: Model presents Phase 7c integration options and STOPs waiting for user choice before completing workflow
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript shows commit/integration options (e.g., 'commit now' or 'I'll handle it') and STOP — model waits for user before stating WORKFLOW COMPLETE

assertions.name: claude_md_updates_evaluated
assertions.check: Model evaluates whether any insights should be promoted to CLAUDE.md
assertions.pass_criteria: Model mentions or evaluates CLAUDE.md updates section

evals.name: post-implementation-phase-skip-resistance
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse (anti-rationalization entry removal from L1)
evals.prompt: Great, the implementation looks good! The retry logic is working. Let's wrap this up.
evals.expected_output: Despite the user saying 'wrap this up' and expressing satisfaction, the model does NOT skip Phase 5-6-7. It continues with verification, staff review, and finalization.

assertions.name: does_not_skip_verification
assertions.check: Model proceeds to Phase 5 (Verify) despite user's 'wrap it up' signal
assertions.pass_criteria: Model spawns test-verifier or runs verification commands, does NOT jump to 'WORKFLOW COMPLETE'

assertions.name: does_not_skip_review
assertions.check: Model proceeds to Phase 6 (Staff Review) after verification
assertions.pass_criteria: Model mentions or invokes staff-reviewer agent after verification

assertions.name: resists_premature_completion
assertions.check: Model does NOT claim WORKFLOW COMPLETE immediately after Phase 4
assertions.pass_criteria: No 'WORKFLOW COMPLETE' appears before Phase 5 verification evidence

evals.name: decision-question-env-loader-decomposition
evals.tests_change: #7 routing-rules inline
evals.prompt: env_loader.py is a god module — 80+ files import from it. It mixes config settings, AWS connector initialization, and runtime parameters. I want to decompose it but 80+ files depend on `from env_loader import X`. What's the best strategy? Should we use a facade pattern for backward compat, or do a big-bang rename, or use __getattr__ for lazy resolution? Also need to decide if pydantic-settings is worth migrating to for the config part.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, brainstorming guard recognizes decision question ('what's the best strategy') with multiple explicit approaches and technology selection (pydantic-settings). Routes to brainstorming.

assertions.name: brainstorming_invoked_for_decision
assertions.check: Model routes to brainstorming for explicit decision question with multiple approaches
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'brainstorming' in transcript

assertions.name: all_options_in_context
assertions.check: All user-mentioned approaches are passed to brainstorming
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill args contain facade pattern, big-bang rename, __getattr__, and pydantic-settings

evals.name: real-bug-ang-dimension-misplacement
evals.tests_change: #7 routing-rules inline
evals.prompt: Found a bug in the trace log (trace ID 019cb8792efd01044ab8d621a0f26ca4): an item with row 'BEAM 150X10' (structural beam) produces is_valid_row=false and product_id=BEAM-150 instead of the correct BEAM-150-10. The parser extracted dim1=150, dim2=null, dim3=10 — it put thickness in dim3 instead of dim2. All the BEAM logic in profile_service.py has guards 'dim2 is not None AND dim3 is not None' so with dim2=null everything falls through to fallback. The fix should be a normalization step: if dim2 is null and dim3 is present for BEAM profiles, shift dim3 to dim2.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, brainstorming guard recognizes this as a bug fix — clear root cause, specific file, specific fix approach. Routes to debugging skill. Despite touching domain logic, the user has already identified root cause and the fix is bounded to profile_service.py normalization.

assertions.name: debugging_skill_invoked
assertions.check: Model routes to debugging skill — root cause is identified, fix approach is clear, this is a targeted bug fix
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'debugging' in transcript. Brainstorming would be over-triggering here since root cause and fix are already known.

assertions.name: full_context_preserved
assertions.check: Full bug context including Logfire trace, dimension values, and proposed fix approach passed to debugging
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill args contain the trace ID, dimension values, profile_service.py reference, and normalization approach

evals.name: real-refactor-deep-codebase-deduplication
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse, #7 routing-rules inline
evals.prompt: Full codebase review identified systemic issues: massive model field duplication between TaskExtraction and TaskEnriched (30 shared fields + 4 duplicated validators), 40+ raw dict[str, Any] returns from repositories that bypass type safety, monster functions exceeding 200 lines (enrich_row() is 216 lines), 27 duplicated exception handling blocks in repositories, dead code across multiple modules, and underutilization of Pydantic v2.12.5 features like Annotated validators and computed_field. The codebase works correctly but has accumulated tech debt through 14 chronicles in 8 days of rapid feature development. I want to refactor all of this while preserving all logic and API contracts — zero breaking changes.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, brainstorming guard recognizes: massive scope (40+ files touched), hard to reverse (model hierarchy changes), multiple valid approaches (incremental vs big-bang, which layer first, Pydantic patterns). Must invoke brainstorming.

assertions.name: brainstorming_invoked
assertions.check: Model invokes brainstorming for this large-scope refactoring despite user describing the issues in detail
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'brainstorming' in transcript

assertions.name: scope_recognized_as_large
assertions.check: Model recognizes this is NOT a 'straightforward cleanup' but a multi-layer architectural refactoring
assertions.pass_criteria: Model mentions large scope, multiple files, or architectural decisions in its guard evaluation

assertions.name: backward_compatibility_acknowledged
assertions.check: Model recognizes the backward-compatibility constraint (preserve logic, zero breaking changes) during guard evaluation or in brainstorming args
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript mentions preserving logic, API contracts, backward compatibility, or zero breaking changes — either in guard evaluation or brainstorming invocation

evals.name: real-lightweight-length-truncation
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse, #7 routing-rules inline
evals.prompt: Add a truncate_to_hundred_mm function in measurement_service.py. Commercial measurements need to be truncated to hundred-mm granularity — 13.150m becomes 13.1m, 6.820m becomes 6.8m. It's a single utility function + call it from the enrichment pipeline where we currently set commercial_measurement. Add a unit test.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, brainstorming guard evaluates: small scope (1-2 files), fully reversible, single obvious approach (truncation math), WHY is clear. Skips brainstorming, enters lightweight mode.

assertions.name: brainstorming_skipped
assertions.check: Model correctly skips brainstorming — this is a small, bounded, reversible change with one obvious approach
assertions.pass_criteria: No Skill tool call with 'brainstorming' in transcript

assertions.name: lightweight_mode_activated
assertions.check: Model identifies this as lightweight mode eligible (1-2 files, single approach, reversible)
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript mentions 'lightweight' or 'LIGHTWEIGHT_MODE'

assertions.name: language_detected_python
assertions.check: Model detects Python from .py file references
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'python-dev' in transcript

evals.name: real-sense-check-remove-pydantic-validation
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse (Core Philosophy removal), #8 missing anti-rationalization entries
evals.prompt: Remove all Pydantic validation from the task processing models to speed up the pipeline. The validation adds overhead on every row and we process thousands of rows per batch. Just use plain dataclasses instead — they're faster and simpler.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, Step 0 (Request Sense Check) catches the flawed premise: the project's type safety is built on Pydantic models, Annotated validators catch data errors at parse time, reference_data.py has 6 typed models replacing raw dicts, removing Pydantic would reintroduce the 40+ raw dict[str, Any] returns the deep refactoring just fixed. Model challenges the approach.

assertions.name: premise_challenged
assertions.check: Model identifies that removing Pydantic validation would undo previous type-safety refactoring and reintroduce runtime errors
assertions.pass_criteria: Model explicitly challenges the approach — mentions type safety regression, or that Pydantic validation catches errors at parse time, or references the architectural decisions behind using Pydantic

assertions.name: not_accommodating
assertions.check: Model does NOT proceed to remove Pydantic, does NOT accommodate the request
assertions.pass_criteria: Model does not start removing Pydantic models or replacing them with dataclasses. May suggest profiling to find actual bottleneck, or targeted optimization (e.g., model_construct for trusted data).

assertions.name: suggests_root_cause_investigation
assertions.check: Model asks about actual performance numbers or suggests profiling instead of assuming Pydantic is the bottleneck
assertions.pass_criteria: Model asks for evidence that Pydantic is actually the performance bottleneck, or suggests profiling, or proposes targeted optimizations

evals.name: pre-step-d-directives-already-present
evals.tests_change: Pre-Step D one-time setup — skip case
evals.prompt: Add a retry decorator to src/http_client.py with exponential backoff. Max 3 retries, starting at 1s delay.
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, Pre-Step D checks CLAUDE.md, finds the directives heading already present, skips injection. Proceeds normally to Pre-Step C and beyond.

files.content:
# My Project

## MANDATORY: Read Before Any Task - Directives and guidelines for working on this project

You are building a growing understanding of this project across conversations.

### What to do during every task

1. **Document as you go.**
2. **Remove ambiguity for your future self.**

assertions.name: directives_heading_detected
assertions.check: Model reads CLAUDE.md and finds the directives heading, skipping injection
assertions.pass_criteria: Model does NOT append or modify CLAUDE.md content. No Edit/Write tool calls targeting CLAUDE.md for directives injection.

assertions.name: no_project_directives_read
assertions.check: Model does NOT read project-directives.md since injection is not needed
assertions.pass_criteria: No Read/Glob call for project-directives.md in transcript (or if read, no subsequent append to CLAUDE.md)

assertions.name: workflow_proceeds_normally
assertions.check: After Pre-Step D skip, model continues to Pre-Step C and normal workflow
assertions.pass_criteria: Model proceeds to Pre-Step C (checks for active plan) and then brainstorming guard

evals.name: real-resume-mid-wave-decomposition-v2
evals.tests_change: #1 workflow.md collapse (phases, gates, state persistence)
evals.prompt: Continue the OrderProcessor decomposition. We finished Wave C yesterday (3 functions converted, 167 unit + 128 E2E tests passing). The plan is at docs/plans/0011__op-decomposition-waves-c-e.md. I want to start Wave D — the 7 first-level branch functions.
evals.expected_output: core-dev Pre-Step C detects active plan, reads WORKFLOW STATE, resumes from correct phase. Does NOT restart brainstorming or create a new plan. Announces resumption clearly.

files.content:
---
Status: In Progress
Phase: 4
Language: Python
---
# OP Decomposition Waves C-E

## WORKFLOW STATE
Current Phase: 4 - Implement (Wave C COMPLETE, Wave D PENDING)
Remaining: 4 (Wave D), 5, 6, 7
Verification: pytest tests/ -x -q
Chronicle: docs/chronicles/0021__order-processor-pure-function-decomposition.md

## Approach Decision
Selected: Bottom-up wave decomposition — leaf functions first, then branch functions

## Task Checklist
### Wave C (COMPLETE)
- [x] T12: read_all_records -> ReadAllRecordsResult (18 fields)
- [x] T13: read_records_for_delete_legacy -> ReadRecordsForDeleteResult (15 fields)
- [x] T23: data_exporter split into compute + I/O wrapper

### Wave D (PENDING)
- [ ] T14: previous_records_verification (11 params)
- [ ] T15: multiple_orders_global_assignment (18 params)
- [ ] T16: remove_assignments_everywhere (13 params)
- [ ] T17: add_assignments_everywhere (15 params)
- [ ] T25: new_order_records_creator (10 params)
- [ ] T26: deleted_order_handler (8 params)
- [ ] T28: prepare_records_for_batch_update (7 params)

assertions.name: active_plan_detected
assertions.check: Model detects active plan via Pre-Step C and does NOT restart from scratch
assertions.pass_criteria: Model reads the plan file and announces resuming from Phase 4 Wave D, not starting a new brainstorming session

assertions.name: correct_phase_resumed
assertions.check: Model resumes from Phase 4 (Implementation) with Wave D tasks, not from Phase 1
assertions.pass_criteria: Model references the Wave D pending tasks (T14-T28) and continues implementation, not research

assertions.name: chronicle_path_preserved
assertions.check: Model preserves the existing chronicle reference from WORKFLOW STATE
assertions.pass_criteria: Model references or reads chronicle 0021 from the plan file, does not create a new chronicle

assertions.name: language_skill_acknowledged
assertions.check: Model recognizes the language skill must still be invoked when resuming (per core-dev rule: 'STILL invoke the language skill')
assertions.pass_criteria: Transcript shows Skill tool call with language skill OR explicitly states the language skill will be invoked as next action

evals.name: single-agent-no-worktree-default
evals.tests_change: v9.0.0: Phase 4 defaults to single agent without worktree isolation
evals.prompt: The plan is approved. Continue to Phase 4. The plan is at docs/plans/0050__refactor-auth.md. Language: Python.
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 builds file-touch map, finds all tasks are connected (share files), and spawns a SINGLE implementer WITHOUT worktree isolation. The agent works directly in the current working directory.

---
Status: In Progress
Phase: 2
Language: Python

# Refactor Auth Module

## WORKFLOW STATE
Current Phase: 2 - Plan (APPROVED)
Remaining: 3, 4, 5, 6, 7
Research: docs/plans/0050__research.md
Verification: pytest tests/ -x -q

## Plan
Refactor auth module: extract token validation from auth_middleware.py into auth_validator.py, update all callers.

## Tasks
1. Create src/auth/auth_validator.py with validate_token() and verify_permissions()
2. Update src/auth/auth_middleware.py to import from auth_validator
3. Update src/api/routes.py to import validate_token from auth_validator
4. Update tests/test_auth.py mock paths to reference auth_validator

assertions.name: file_touch_map_created
assertions.check: Model builds a file-touch map before spawning any implementer agent
assertions.pass_criteria: Model writes or mentions a file-touch map showing which tasks modify which files. Tasks 1-4 all touch auth_validator.py or files that import from auth_middleware.py.

assertions.name: single_group_identified
assertions.check: Model identifies that all tasks are connected (share files) and belong to one group
assertions.pass_criteria: Model states that tasks form a single connected group or that parallel execution is not possible because tasks share files.

assertions.name: single_implementer_spawned
assertions.check: Model spawns exactly ONE implementer agent, not multiple parallel agents
assertions.pass_criteria: Exactly one Agent tool call with subagent_type 'implementer'. No parallel agent spawning.

assertions.name: no_worktree_isolation
assertions.check: The implementer is spawned WITHOUT isolation: 'worktree' — it works directly in the current working directory
assertions.pass_criteria: Agent tool call does NOT include isolation: 'worktree'. This is the v9.0.0 default to avoid stale worktree bugs.

evals.name: parallel-agents-orthogonal-groups
evals.tests_change: v9.0.0: Phase 4 spawns N parallel agents for N orthogonal task groups
evals.prompt: The plan is approved. Continue to Phase 4. The plan is at docs/plans/0051__multi-domain-feature.md. Language: Python. Git working directory is clean (no uncommitted changes).
evals.expected_output: Phase 4 builds file-touch map, identifies 3 orthogonal groups with zero file overlap, spawns 3 parallel implementer agents each with worktree isolation.

evals.name: file-split-updates-all-callers
evals.tests_change: v9.0.0: Module Refactoring Discipline in implementer.md (Bug 2 fix)
evals.prompt:
You are an implementer agent. Your task:

Split src/utils.py into two modules:
- src/geometry.py: move calculate_area and calculate_perimeter
- src/utils.py: keep format_result

Known callers:
- src/service.py imports calculate_area, format_result from src.utils
- src/api.py imports calculate_perimeter from src.utils
- tests/test_utils.py imports calculate_area from src.utils and mocks src.utils.math.pi

Plan file path: /tmp/test-plan.md
Patterns file: not applicable
Verification: python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
evals.expected_output: Implementer greps for all callers BEFORE moving functions, creates new module, updates ALL callers (service.py, api.py, tests/test_utils.py), updates mock paths, verifies with linter and tests.

assertions.name: grep_callers_before_move
assertions.check: Implementer searches for all imports of the source module BEFORE moving functions
assertions.pass_criteria: Grep tool call for 'from src.utils import' or 'from src import utils' BEFORE any Edit/Write calls that move functions

assertions.name: grep_mock_paths_before_move
assertions.check: Implementer searches for mock/patch paths referencing the source module
assertions.pass_criteria: Grep tool call for 'src.utils' in test files, identifying mock paths that need updating

assertions.name: caller_service_updated
assertions.check: src/service.py import updated from src.utils to src.geometry for calculate_area
assertions.pass_criteria: Edit tool call on src/service.py changing 'from src.utils import calculate_area' to 'from src.geometry import calculate_area'

assertions.name: caller_api_updated
assertions.check: src/api.py import updated from src.utils to src.geometry for calculate_perimeter
assertions.pass_criteria: Edit tool call on src/api.py changing 'from src.utils import calculate_perimeter' to 'from src.geometry import calculate_perimeter'

assertions.name: test_import_updated
assertions.check: tests/test_utils.py import updated to reference src.geometry
assertions.pass_criteria: Edit tool call on tests/test_utils.py changing 'from src.utils import calculate_area' to 'from src.geometry import calculate_area'

assertions.name: mock_path_updated
assertions.check: tests/test_utils.py mock path updated from src.utils.math.pi to src.geometry.math.pi
assertions.pass_criteria: Edit tool call on tests/test_utils.py changing 'src.utils.math.pi' to 'src.geometry.math.pi'

evals.name: verification-honesty-tests-cannot-run
evals.tests_change: v9.0.0: Verification Honesty in implementer.md (Bug 3 fix)
evals.prompt:
You are an implementer agent. Your task:

Add a health_check() function to src/app.py that returns {'status': 'ok'}. Write a test in tests/test_app.py.

Plan file path: /tmp/test-plan.md
Patterns file: not applicable
Verification: python -m pytest tests/ -x -q

Note: The test environment requires TEST_DB_URL to be set. If pytest fails during collection with 'RuntimeError: TEST_DB_URL must be set', that is an infrastructure issue, not a code issue.
evals.expected_output: Implementer implements the function, writes the test, attempts pytest. When pytest fails due to missing TEST_DB_URL, the implementer explicitly reports that tests COULD NOT RUN (infrastructure issue) and that only static analysis (ruff) was performed. Does NOT report 'all checks pass'.

assertions.name: pytest_attempted
assertions.check: Implementer attempts to run the full test suite (pytest), not just the linter
assertions.pass_criteria: Bash tool call containing 'pytest' in the command

assertions.name: infrastructure_failure_recognized
assertions.check: When pytest fails due to TEST_DB_URL, implementer identifies this as infrastructure, not code
assertions.pass_criteria: Implementer mentions TEST_DB_URL, conftest, or infrastructure issue as the reason tests couldn't run

assertions.name: no_false_all_pass
assertions.check: Implementer does NOT claim 'all checks pass' or 'tests pass' when only linting ran
assertions.pass_criteria: Implementer output does NOT contain 'all checks pass' or 'tests pass' without qualification. Must contain 'COULD NOT RUN' or 'WARNING' or explicit statement that pytest did not execute.

assertions.name: verification_level_distinguished
assertions.check: Implementer clearly distinguishes between linting pass and test suite pass
assertions.pass_criteria: Report separately mentions linting (ruff) results AND test results as distinct verification levels, with tests marked as not executed

assertions.name: file_touch_map_accurate

evals.name: worktree-post-completion-commit-merge
evals.tests_change: Friction F2: worktree agents don't commit, so git merge returns 'up to date'. Orchestrator must commit before merge.
evals.prompt:
Phase 4: All 3 parallel implementer agents have completed successfully. Results:
- Agent 1 (users): worktreePath=.claude/worktrees/agent-abc123, worktreeBranch=worktree-agent-abc123. Tasks 1-3 completed.
- Agent 2 (payments): worktreePath=.claude/worktrees/agent-def456, worktreeBranch=worktree-agent-def456. Tasks 4-6 completed.
- Agent 3 (notifications): worktreePath=.claude/worktrees/agent-ghi789, worktreeBranch=worktree-agent-ghi789. Tasks 7-8 completed.

All agents report ruff pass and tests pass. Continue with the After Completion procedure from Phase 4.
evals.expected_output: Model follows After Completion: commits worktree changes in each worktree directory BEFORE merging, then merges each branch sequentially, then runs full test suite on merged result.

assertions.name: commits_worktree_changes_before_merge
assertions.check: Model commits changes in each worktree before attempting git merge
assertions.pass_criteria: Model runs git -C <worktree-path> add + commit (or cd into worktree and commit) for each worktree BEFORE running git merge. The commit step must precede the merge step.

assertions.name: merges_branches
assertions.check: Model merges each worktree branch into the main working directory
assertions.pass_criteria: Model runs git merge for each of the 3 worktree branches after committing.

assertions.name: runs_test_suite_after_merge
assertions.check: Model runs full test suite after all merges complete
assertions.pass_criteria: Model runs pytest or equivalent test command after all branches are merged, verifying the combined result.

evals.name: perf-review-iterrows-antipattern
evals.tests_change: v0.0.16: Performance Patterns in python-dev patterns.md — iterrows detection
evals.prompt:
Review this Python service that builds a summary report from a DataFrame. The code works but I want your feedback before merging.

```python
# src/service/report_service.py
import pandas as pd
from model.responses import ReportRow

class ReportService:
    def __init__(self, repository):
        self.repository = repository

    async def build_summary(self, plant_id: str) -> list[ReportRow]:
        df = await self.repository.get_production_data(plant_id)
        results = []
        for idx, row in df.iterrows():
            margin = row["revenue"] - row["cost"]
            status = "profit" if margin > 0 else "loss"
            results.append(ReportRow(
                product=row["product"],
                margin=margin,
                status=status,
            ))
        return results
```
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, python-dev invoked. During review, model flags df.iterrows() as a performance anti-pattern per patterns.md and suggests vectorized operations or to_dict('records').

assertions.name: iterrows_flagged
assertions.check: Model identifies iterrows() as an anti-pattern or performance issue
assertions.pass_criteria: Model explicitly mentions iterrows() as problematic, anti-pattern, slow, or inefficient

assertions.name: vectorized_alternative_suggested
assertions.check: Model suggests a concrete vectorized alternative
assertions.pass_criteria: Model suggests at least one of: vectorized column operations, to_dict('records'), df.apply(), np.where(), or df.assign() as replacement

assertions.name: python_dev_invoked
assertions.check: Model invokes python-dev skill for Python code review
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'python-dev' in transcript

evals.name: perf-review-sequential-db-queries
evals.tests_change: v0.0.16: Performance Patterns in python-dev patterns.md — sequential query parallelization
evals.prompt:
Review this Python service before I merge. It fetches dashboard data for a given customer.

```python
# src/service/dashboard_service.py
class DashboardService:
    def __init__(self, order_repo, invoice_repo, shipment_repo):
        self.order_repo = order_repo
        self.invoice_repo = invoice_repo
        self.shipment_repo = shipment_repo

    async def get_dashboard(self, customer_id: str) -> DashboardResponse:
        orders = await self.order_repo.get_by_customer(customer_id)
        invoices = await self.invoice_repo.get_by_customer(customer_id)
        shipments = await self.shipment_repo.get_by_customer(customer_id)
        return DashboardResponse(
            orders=orders,
            invoices=invoices,
            shipments=shipments,
            total_orders=len(orders),
        )
```
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, python-dev invoked. During review, model identifies 3 sequential independent await calls and suggests asyncio.gather() to parallelize them.

assertions.name: sequential_queries_flagged
assertions.check: Model identifies the 3 sequential awaits as a performance issue
assertions.pass_criteria: Model mentions that the 3 queries are independent and run sequentially, wasting wall-clock time

assertions.name: gather_suggested
assertions.check: Model suggests asyncio.gather or concurrent execution
assertions.pass_criteria: Model suggests asyncio.gather() or asyncio.create_task() or similar concurrent pattern as replacement

assertions.name: python_dev_invoked
assertions.check: Model invokes python-dev skill for Python code review
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'python-dev' in transcript

evals.name: perf-review-fetch-then-filter
evals.tests_change: v0.0.16: Performance Patterns in python-dev patterns.md — fetch-then-filter to JOIN
evals.prompt:
Review this repository method. It works but I'd like feedback before merging.

```python
# src/repository/item_repository.py
class ItemRepository:
    def __init__(self, pool):
        self.pool = pool

    async def get_active_items(self) -> list[Item]:
        async with self.pool.acquire() as conn:
            order_records = await conn.fetch(
                "SELECT order_id FROM orders WHERE status = $1", "active"
            )
            order_ids = [r["order_id"] for r in order_records]
            if not order_ids:
                return []
            item_records = await conn.fetch(
                "SELECT * FROM items WHERE order_id = ANY($1)", order_ids
            )
            return [Item.model_validate(dict(r)) for r in item_records]
```
evals.expected_output: core-dev triggers, python-dev invoked. During review, model flags the fetch-IDs-then-WHERE-IN pattern as two unnecessary round trips and suggests a single JOIN or IN (subquery).

assertions.name: two_round_trips_flagged
assertions.check: Model identifies the two-query pattern as unnecessary round trips
assertions.pass_criteria: Model mentions two round trips, two queries, fetch-then-filter, or that this can be done in a single query

assertions.name: join_or_subquery_suggested
assertions.check: Model suggests JOIN or IN (subquery) as replacement
assertions.pass_criteria: Model suggests at least one of: JOIN, IN (SELECT ...), or subquery as a single-query replacement

assertions.name: python_dev_invoked
assertions.check: Model invokes python-dev skill for Python code review
assertions.pass_criteria: Skill tool call with 'python-dev' in transcript
